title: Lemony Glazed White Cabbage Corn-Husk Wrap
description: A sour, juicy Japanese dish featuring white cabbage wrapped in corn-husk, glazed with a lactic acid-based sauce that is lacto-vegetarian-friendly.
instructions: Wash the white cabbage and remove any damaged leaves.
Core the cabbage by cutting off about a third of its top to create a flat surface for wrapping.
Blanch the corn-husk in boiling water for 30 seconds, then remove with tongs. Lay out on a clean kitchen towel and let dry thoroughly.
Place one piece of blanched corn-husk on a chopping board and place a cabbage leaf over it. Wrap the cabbage around the corn-husk in pleats, securing with skewers if needed.
In a small bowl, mix together lemon juice, glutinous rice wine vinegar (takuan-soya), sweet soy sauce, sesame oil, sugar, and salt to taste. Pour the mixture evenly over each wrapped cabbage leaf.
Place the prepared corn-husk wraps on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper. Bake in a 200°C preheated oven for 15 minutes or until the cabbage is tender and slightly caramelized.
Serve immediately, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
